title: Air defense tower
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Otheruses|Air Defense|more=Tower}}
[[File:ADTowerRZ-0031.jpg|thumb|300px|An Air Defense Tower erected within [[RZ-0031]].]]
{{Quote|That's an air defense tower. The whole city's filled with them.|Captain Riiken, to the Jedi Exile|audio=AirDefenseTower.flac|<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''</ref>}}
An '''Air Defense Tower''' was a fixed defensive weapons emplacement that was often found throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Mounting two Gatling-style rapid-fire [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]], these potent emplacements were usually erected in areas with a good line of sight. AD Towers were capable of targeting many different kinds of airborne threats, ranging from marauding [[Swoop/Legends|swoop bikes]] to low-flying [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] with relative ease.

==History==
A few of these towers were to be found within the [[Telos IV|Telosian]] [[Restoration Zone]]s operated by [[Czerka Arms/Legends|Czerka Corporation]], particularly in [[RZ-0031]]. After coming off the worst in an encounter with such an installation, [[Atton Rand]] recalled seeing [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] bases that sported similar types of defensive weaponry. Air defense towers were found on other worlds as well, utilizing different types of [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] and [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]] depending on the emplacement's projected use and the availability of component technology. The Great Walled City of [[Iziz/Legends|Iziz]], which was the only center of civilization on the jungle world of [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]], also boasted a large network of [[Iziz defense tower|air defense towers]] that had been built up over the city's first millennium of existence. Their primary role was to defend the city and its inhabitants from the [[Beast Riders]], criminals who had been cast out of the city and forced to live in the wilds, and their great winged [[Drexl]]s.

The operation of air defense towers and their construction differed greatly across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Many of these types of defenses used computer-aided targeting systems, yet still depended on [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] beings to act as [[Gunner/Legends|gunners]] to select and prosecute targets. Several types were outfitted for complete autonomous operation, freeing personnel for other duties or to protect an abandoned location against unauthorized access, as in the case of the towers located in RZ-0031. Many thousands of years later, air defense towers would see further use during the decline of the Republic. Factions such as the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]], the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] all used varying models of these devices. By this time, however, laser-armed towers had largely given way to projectile weapons-based emplacements, the more advanced of which fired ordnance that could home in on their targets, despite their use of evasive action.

==Czerka turrets==
'''Czerka air-defense turrets''' were turrets planted by [[Czerka Arms/Legends|Czerka Corporation]] to protect Czerka bases from trespassing starships. When the [[Meetra Surik|Jedi Exile]] and her companions ventured onto the surface of the planet [[Telos IV]] in search of her missing ship, ''[[Ebon Hawk]]'', a multi-barreled Czerka air-defense turret shot their [[Ministry-class orbital shuttle|shuttle]] down. Luckily, the Exile's former technician from the [[Mandalorian Wars]], [[Bao-Dur]], was able to pull the Exile and her companions to safety.
